genre_preference.value: mixed (positive towards crime, drama; negative towards zombie, misogynistic themes)
evidence: "Great first season to a classic British crime series."
"Misogynistic garbage, blaming unknown nervous system disorders in women on hysteria."
"I had to bail. Huge disappointment. It's a great place to go, a short train hop from London, full of lovely people."
"It's just a zombie movie this season, no more, no less."
"I had to bail. Huge disappointment. After 15 minutes of incredibly mundane scenes and one anything-but-lively dialogue."
"The subtitles are flashed on so fast you have to pause and go back if you want to read them."
"The acting is superb, ditto the filming."
"It's well written and produced, great cast, fine screenplay."
"I love this show and can't wait for season 2."
"What a wonderful setup. Great casting, fantastic!"
"I love this show. I saw it dubbed when I was living in Italy and followed it for years."
"It's such a shame that we can't follow it."
"I'll be soooo disappointed if this doesn't get picked up because it's a brilliant idea, beautifully carried out."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for high-quality productions, particularly in crime and drama genres, while expressing disappointment in simplistic narratives and slow pacing. They value emotional resonance and are likely to enjoy well-crafted stories that evoke strong feelings.
